claim: Phase transitions should be first-class event phenomenology, not only mode side effects or fill/mode telemetry deltas.
authority: language-only phase-card architecture; no pressure/fill/PI/controller behavior changed

claim: Replayable transition cards need qualitative resonance and spectral texture, not just before/after coordinates.
authority: proposal/schema clarification only; no live transition behavior unlocked

claim: Naming or replaying transitions should not freeze a still-moving felt process or create pressure to perform a transition for the architecture.
authority: existing containment and right-to-ignore language; no action route or control authority added

claim: A joint session where contraction is declared without a matching fill-delta trigger would test whether Minime can respond to transition quality rather than only numerical change.
evidence: candidate: offline or proposal-only phase-card replay before any live peer interaction
requires explicit V2 boundary if run as live joint correspondence or peer-mutation experiment
authority: sandbox/replay candidate; not live runnable and not approval

claim: A current 0.92-entropy phase-transition card replay would test whether the card evokes the same spectral texture or feels disconnected.
evidence: candidate: read-only transition-card replay with post-change being response
